LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOSEPH CASSIDY AND CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINTS IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY. (CCP 425.16(F).) CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOSEPH CASSIDY AND CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC. ("CROSS-DEFENDANTS") HAVE NOT PROVIDED ANY BASIS FOR THE COURT TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO CONSIDER THE MOTIONS ON THE MERITS. CROSS-DEFENDANTS HAVE ANSWERED THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS. (ON JULY 30, 2012, MR. FLOOD SERVED HIS CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR ANSWER ON AUGUST 21, 2012. ON SEPTEMBER 28, 2012, 973 MARKET ASSOCIATES, LLC, MR. O'DONOGHUE, AND MR. MANNING, MR. MURPHY AND MS. QUIGLEY SEPARATELY SERVED BY MAIL THEIR FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINTS, AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR ANSWER ON NOVEMBER 9, 2012. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINTS FILED ON SEPTEMBER 28, 2012 DID NOT SUBSTANTIVELY CHANGE THE ALLEGATIONS AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANTS. (OPPOSITION, LEON DECL., PAR.13.) ON OCTOBER 4, 2012, MR. WALSH SERVED BY MAIL HIS CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR ANSWER ON NOVEMBER 9, 2012.) CROSS-DEFENDANTS FILED THE INSTANT MOTION ON JANUARY 29, 2013. CROSS-DEFENDANTS DID NOT REQUEST PERMISSION TO EXTEND THE TIME TO FILE THIS MOTION OR PROVIDE A SUFFICIENT EXPLANATION FOR THEIR DELAY IN FILING THIS MOTION IN THEIR MOVING PAPERS OR REPLY. NEITHER THE DEMURRERS BY OTHER CROSS-DEFENDANTS OR THE APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE ON JANUARY 14, 2013 PROVIDES AN ADEQUATE BASIS TO EXTEND THE TIME UNDER CCP 425.16(F) FOR CROSS-DEFENDANTS TO FILE THIS MOTION. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TONY MANNING AND PARAIC O'DONOGHUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 16, 2016 THE SELLERS MUST FILE AND SERVE A MEMORANDUM OF COSTS WORKSHEET (JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM MC-011) OR EQUIVALENT DOCUMENT ITEMIZING AND SEPARATELY NUMBERING EACH ITEM OF COST WHICH THE SELLERS ARE CLAIMING AND PROVIDING ALL THE INFORMATION CALLED FOR IN THE WORKSHEET. WHILE THE WORKSHEET IS TECHNICALLY AN OPTIONAL FORM, WHEN, AS HERE, A PARTY CLAIMS A SUBSTANTIAL AMOUNT OF COSTS FOR MANY DIFFERENT ITEMS OCCURRING OVER AN EXTENDED PERIOD OF TIME, AN ITEMIZATION AS CALLED FOR IN THE WORKSHEET OR SOMETHING PROVIDING EQUIVALENT INFORMATION IS ESSENTIAL FOR THE OPPOSING PARTY TO BE ABLE TO EVALUATE WHAT COSTS HE BELIEVES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE AND TO EXPLAIN TO THE PARTY CLAIMING COSTS AND THE COURT WHY HE BELIEVES THOSE ARE NOT RECOVERABLE. NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 1, 2016 MR. MANNING AND MR. O'DONOGHUE MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS RESPONDING TO THE WORKSHEET. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHRISTOPHER FLOOD'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 16, 2016 THE SELLERS MUST FILE AND SERVE A MEMORANDUM OF COSTS WORKSHEET (JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM MC-011) OR EQUIVALENT DOCUMENT ITEMIZING AND SEPARATELY NUMBERING EACH ITEM OF COST WHICH THE SELLERS ARE CLAIMING AND PROVIDING ALL THE INFORMATION CALLED FOR IN THE WORKSHEET. WHILE THE WORKSHEET IS TECHNICALLY AN OPTIONAL FORM, WHEN, AS HERE, A PARTY CLAIMS A SUBSTANTIAL AMOUNT OF COSTS FOR MANY DIFFERENT ITEMS OCCURRING OVER AN EXTENDED PERIOD OF TIME, AN ITEMIZATION AS CALLED FOR IN THE WORKSHEET OR SOMETHING PROVIDING EQUIVALENT INFORMATION IS ESSENTIAL FOR THE OPPOSING PARTY TO BE ABLE TO EVALUATE WHAT COSTS HE BELIEVES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE AND TO EXPLAIN TO THE PARTY CLAIMING COSTS AND THE COURT WHY HE BELIEVES THOSE ARE NOT RECOVERABLE. NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 1, 2016 MR. FLOOD MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS RESPONDING TO THE WORKSHEET. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Continued To Dec-15-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Owen Rescher of Rescher Law, P.C. telephone number 415-722-8923 for Cross-Complainants Tony Manning and Paraic O'Donoghue.
John O'Connor of O'Connor and Associates telephone number 415-693-9960 for Cross-Complainant Christopher Flood.
Jennifer Supman of Mannig & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP telephone number 415-400-0801 for all of the Seller Cross-Defendants.

The Court modified its tentative ruling pursuant to the agreement of the parties. Cross-complainant Christopher Flood's motion to strike or tax costs is continued to December 15, 2016. No later than November 16, 2016 the Sellers must file and serve a memorandum of costs worksheet (Judicial Council form MC-011) or equivalent document itemizing and separately numbering each item of cost which the Sellers are claiming and providing all the information called for in the worksheet. While the worksheet is technically an optional form, when, as here, a party claims a substantial amount of costs for many different items occurring over an extended period of time, an itemization as called for in the worksheet or something providing equivalent information is essential for the opposing party to be able to evaluate what costs he believes are not recoverable and to explain to the party claiming costs and the court why he believes those are not recoverable. No later than December 1, 2016 Mr. Flood may file supplemental reply papers responding to the worksheet.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Appearances: Owen Rescher of Rescher Law, P.C. telephone number 415-722-8923 for Cross-Complainants Tony Manning and Paraic O'Donoghue.
John O'Connor of O'Connor and Associates telephone number 415-693-9960 for Cross-Complainant Christopher Flood.
Jennifer Supman of Mannig & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP telephone number 415-400-0801 for all of the Seller Cross-Defendants.

Mr. Rescher stated that he will review the trial exhibits by the end of the day. The Court modified its tentative ruling pursuant to the agreement of the parties. Cross-complainants Tony Manning and Paraic O'Donoghue's motion to strike or tax costs is continued to December 15, 2016. No later than November 16, 2016 the Sellers must file and serve a memorandum of costs worksheet (Judicial Council form MC-011) or equivalent document itemizing and separately numbering each item of cost which the Sellers are claiming and providing all the information called for in the worksheet. While the worksheet is technically an optional form, when, as here, a party claims a substantial amount of costs for many different items occurring over an extended period of time, an itemization as called for in the worksheet or something providing equivalent information is essential for the opposing party to be able to evaluate what costs he believes are not recoverable and to explain to the party claiming costs and the court why he believes those are not recoverable. No later than December 1, 2016 Mr. Manning and Mr. O'Donoghue may file supplemental reply papers responding to the worksheet.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Jennifer L. Supman, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (415.217.6990) for Cross-Defendants / Opposing Parties LEO CASSIDY, as an individual and as Trustee of the LEO & DEIRDRE CASSIDY FAMILY TRUST, JAMES NUNEMACHER, DEIRDRE CASSIDY, LJ SF INVESTMENTS LLC, and 973 MARKET STREET LLC (referred to as "Local Sellers");
Edward Egan Smith, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415.421.3400) for Plaintiff PERFORMING ARTS, LLC; Coss-Defendants JOSEPH P. CASSIDY and CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC.;
John O'Connor, Esq. of O'Connor & Associates (415.680.7621) appearing by telephone (at 10:25 AM) for Defendant / Cross Complainant CHRISTOPHER FLOOD.

(10:00 AM: By direction of the Court, the clerk calls and emails counsel John O'Connor in an attempt to have counsel join this morning's hearing. The clerk does not receive a response back by telephone nor by email from Mr. O'Connor.)

10:02 AM: The Court, court personnel, and respective counsel are present in open court for hearing re Ex Parte Application by Tony Manning for an Order Correcting Erroneous March 3, 2015 Summary Judgment Order and Erroneous April 16, 2015 Judgment.

The Court proposes the changes to the March 3, 2015 order and April 16, 2015 Judgment as fully reflected in the court reporter's notes. The parties are in agreement. The Court orders Mr. Rescher to prepare and submit a proposed amended order and judgment.

10:12 AM: Off the record, the Court and respective counsel discuss outstanding issues.
(The clerk calls Jessica Shafer, an associate of Mr. O'Connor to join this morning's session. Ms. Shafer defers and will contact Mr. O'Connor on behalf of the Court.)

10:32 AM: On the record, Mr. O'Conner joins this morning's impromptu status conference by telephone. Counsel restate their appearances and convene discussions of the local seller's Leo Cassidy' et al.'s MSJ scheduling. The parties agree to the following deposition dates by Ms. Supman: AUG 18 for Mr. Flood; AUG 19 for Mr. O'Donoghue; AUG 21 for Mr. Manning.

10:40 AM: Break.

10:44 AM: Court reconvenes with all parties present. Scheduling discussions resume. The parties stipulate to the following scheduling:
AUG-31-2015: filing by hand of MSJ by the 'Local Sellers'
OCT-05-2015: filing by hand of opposition
OCT-14-2015: filing of reply
OCT-19-2015, 9AM: hearing of MSJ, and further CMC to discuss Jury Questionnaire and MIL scheduling.
The parties further stipulate to use a rough transcript for preparation of the MSJ.
NOV-10-2015: juror questionnaires will be passed out
NOV-12-2015: MIL hearing
NOV-13-2015: voir dire
NOV-25-2015: dark
NOV-26-27-2015: holiday, closed

No extension of disclosure dates. October 30, 2015 is the disclosure trigger date. Last day for expert discovery and motions is November 3, 2015.

The parties discuss a concern regarding the depositions of Nunebacker and Joseph Cassidy.
A further Case Management Conference is set for SEP-01-2015 at 9:00 AM. CMC statements are not required. Appearances will be required on SEP-01-2015 at 9:00 a.m. unless all parties agree to cancel.

11:02 AM: Hearing concludes.

Off Calendar May-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JANET M. BRAYER, TEL. NO. 445-9555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. FRANK M. LA FLEUR & ATTY. JENNIFER SUPMAN APPEARING FOR LEO CASSIDY, JAMES NUNEMACHER ET. AL.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF 973 MARKET ASSOCIATES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED's Mtn For Protective Order Relieving 973 Market Association Llc'S Obligation To Respond To Deft The Chris James Corp., James Nunemacher, X-Deft'S Leo Cassidy, Deirdre Cassidy, Lj Sf Investments Llc, 973 Market St Llc'S Judicial Council Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Admissions, Set One - The motion for a Protective Order is Denied. The discovery propounded include contention interrogatories and seek documents and admissions that each cross-defendant is entitled to receive pursuant to C.C.P. 2030.010, 2031.010 and 2033.010, et al. If the discovery responses are the same for each of the cross-defendants as the moving papers contend, it would be just as easy to provide the same response in each set as in the first set with verifications from each separate cross-complainant. The moving party will have 60 days from the date of the order to provide responses to the discovery. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Arlene Barton; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion To Strike X-Complaints
Off Calendar Feb-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. GARY A. ANGEL, TEL. NO. 788-5935 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EDWARD EGAN SMITH OF STEYER LOWENTHAL BOODROOKAS & SMITH, LLP, TEL. NO. 421-3400 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT CENTRIX & CASSIDY;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. MAZZOCONE, TEL. NO. 399-0800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT 973 MARKET ASSOCIATE, LLC;
ATTY. CATHERINE M. LEON, TEL. NO. 259-6447 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS O' DONOGUE, MANNING, MURPHY, QUIGLEY & WALSH.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOSEPH CASSIDY AND CENTRIX BUILDERS, INC.'S Motion To Strike Cross-Complaints is Denied. The motion is untimely. (CCP 425.16(f).) Cross-Defendants Joseph Cassidy and Centrix Builders, Inc. ("Cross-Defendants") have not provided any basis for the court to exercise its discretion to consider the motions on the merits. Cross-Defendants have answered the Cross-Complaints. (On July 30, 2012, Mr. Flood served his Cross-Complaint, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on August 21, 2012. On September 28, 2012, 973 Market Associates, LLC, Mr. O'Donoghue, and Mr. Manning, Mr. Murphy and Ms. Quigley separately served by mail their First Amended Cross-Complaints, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on November 9, 2012. The First Amended Cross-Complaints filed on September 28, 2012 did not substantively change the allegations against Cross-Defendants. (Opposition, Leon Decl., par.13.) On October 4, 2012, Mr. Walsh served by mail his Cross-Complaint, and Cross-Defendants filed their answer on November 9, 2012.) Cross-Defendants filed the instant motion on January 29, 2013. Cross-Defendants did not request permission to extend the time to file this motion or provide a sufficient explanation for their delay in filing this motion in their moving papers or reply. Neither the demurrers by other cross-defendants or the appointment of a referee on January 14, 2013 provides an adequate basis to extend the time under CCP 425.16(f) for Cross-Defendants to file this motion. Request for attorney fees denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
